introSection: Vertigo is a steel roller coaster currently located at Zoomarine in Rome, Lazio, Italy. It was designed by Anton Schwarzkopf and Werner Stengel, and is a portable model.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The coaster opened at Pleasureland Southport in 1985, under loan from Bembom Rides.

After returning back to them from Luneur Park after the 2007 season, Parkware Limited sold this coaster to Irish showman William Bird, which travelled Ireland with it. He loaned it to Tramore Amusement Park in 2008 until 2009 when he sold it to Zoomarine where it reopened on 27 March 2010 as Vertigo.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Brown track and supports, with the boxed spine on the vertical loop being aqua. The ride used to feature red track and white supports.

fullText:
Vertigo is a steel roller coaster currently located at Zoomarine in Rome, Lazio, Italy. It was designed by Anton Schwarzkopf and Werner Stengel, and is a portable model.

The coaster opened at Pleasureland Southport in 1985, under loan from Bembom Rides.

After returning back to them from Luneur Park after the 2007 season, Parkware Limited sold this coaster to Irish showman William Bird, which travelled Ireland with it. He loaned it to Tramore Amusement Park in 2008 until 2009 when he sold it to Zoomarine where it reopened on 27 March 2010 as Vertigo.



Brown track and supports, with the boxed spine on the vertical loop being aqua. The ride used to feature red track and white supports.
